definition: The securitization pipeline is bifurcating from traditional RMBS (agency origination down from ~$4.4T to ~$1.8T) toward esoteric ABS (data center ABS, whole-business securitization, insurance-linked securities) with 5-15 year performance histories vs. 40+ for RMBS. This represents a structural increase in model risk, though the demographic component is second-order.
evidence: Agency MBS origination down from ~$4.4T (2021) to ~$1.8T run rate
CLO managers rotating into data center ABS, whole-business securitization, insurance-linked securities
Demographic effect on traditional RMBS pipeline is ~20-25% narrowing
open_questions: How should model risk for esoteric ABS be priced without long performance histories?
Will regulatory capital rules accelerate or slow the bifurcation?
Is the demographic contribution genuinely second-order or underappreciated?
